sanskrit:
पयात्नाद्यतमानस्तु योगी संशुद्धकिल्बिषः।
अनेकजन्मसंसिद्धस्ततो याति परां गतिम्॥
transliteration: prayatnād yatamānas tu yogī saṃśuddha-kilbiṣaḥ / aneka-janma-saṃsiddhas tato yāti parāṃ gatim //
literal_meaning: By sustained effort the yogī, cleansed of impurities, perfected through many births, then attains the supreme state.
contextual_meaning: Lasting change is a long-arc project: persistent effort plus self-purification across lifetimes culminates in the highest psychological integration.
psychological_state: Post-setback resilience: the client re-frames relapse as data, not defeat, and re-commits to the path.
trigger_scenario: After months of therapy, a client relapses into panic attacks; instead of quitting, they see it as one more lap in a marathon toward healing.
anchor_text: Keep purifying yourself; perfection ripens over lifetimes of steady effort.
